questions.explanation: Android Studio is the official IDE for Android development, providing code editing, build tools integration, emulators, and debugging.

options.A: Designing and building Android apps in an IDE
options.B: Hosting Android apps in the cloud
options.D: Encrypting user data on a device

questions.question: What is Android Studio primarily used for in mobile app development?

questions.explanation: The Android SDK includes platform APIs, build tools, and platform tools (like adb) needed to build and package apps.

options.A: Android platform tools and build tools

questions.question: Which component is part of the Android SDK and is required to compile an Android app?

questions.explanation: Kotlin uses 'val' for read-only references and 'var' for mutable references.
questions.question: In Kotlin, which keyword declares a read-only variable (cannot be reassigned)?

questions.explanation: Debuggers help developers pause execution, set breakpoints, inspect state, and diagnose issues.

options.A: To step through code, inspect variables, and find defects
options.B: To automatically generate UI designs from sketches
options.C: To publish apps to the Play Store

questions.question: What is the main purpose of debug software in an IDE?

questions.explanation: @Composable functions declare UI; Compose recomposes them when observed state changes.

options.A: To describe UI by emitting a UI tree based on state
options.B: To compile Kotlin code into native C++
options.C: To define database schemas for Room
options.D: To perform network requests on the main thread

questions.question: In Jetpack Compose, what is the primary role of a @Composable function?

questions.explanation: The ViewModel holds and manages UI-related data/state and survives configuration changes, exposing state to the View.
questions.question: In MVVM on Android, which layer should contain UI state and expose it to the UI (Activity/Fragment/Compose)?

questions.explanation: Retrofit uses OkHttp for HTTP calls and a converter (like Moshi/Gson) to map JSON to Kotlin data classes.
questions.question: You need to call a REST API using Retrofit and parse JSON into Kotlin objects. Which combination is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: Launching in viewModelScope ties the work to the ViewModel lifecycle, enabling structured concurrency and automatic cancellation when the ViewModel is cleared.

options.B: In a global scope (GlobalScope.launch) so it always finishes
options.C: On the main thread without coroutines

questions.question: A Compose screen triggers a network request when a button is clicked. The request must be cancellable when the user leaves the screen. Where should you launch the coroutine to best match lifecycle and cancellation needs?

questions.explanation: A repository mediates between Room and network; Room provides offline persistence, and WorkManager can schedule reliable background sync.

options.A: Repository pattern with Room as a local source of truth and a sync layer (e.g., WorkManager) to update from network
options.B: Store all data only in memory inside the Activity to avoid disk I/O
options.C: Make every screen fetch directly from Retrofit and disable caching
options.D: Use only SharedPreferences for all relational data and run sync on the main thread

questions.question: Your app must work offline and sync when connectivity returns. Which Android Jetpack Architecture Components-based approach best supports this requirement?

questions.explanation: Git is a version control system used for configuration management: branching, merging, history tracking, and rollback.
questions.question: A team is building a software prototype and wants reliable collaboration, history, and the ability to revert changes. Which tool is most appropriate for software configuration management?
